author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: Capstone: zero the SDK import-boundary baseline (91 entries) — flip remaining CLI/MCP private-core imports to SDK primitives and harden the ratchet into a hard boundary gate

pm-8778 shipped the ratchet gate; scripts/release/sdk-import-boundary-baseline.json currently allows 91 private src/core imports from src/cli + src/mcp. Each SDK promotion item (pm-rjqr/98cz/zwpp/je50/4a7m/3mna/oxrw/oslr) flips its own domain's CLI handlers, but no child owns the terminal state: (1) residual imports not covered by any single domain promotion, (2) the MCP-layer flip — promotion ACs name CLI handlers only while the epic AC requires src/mcp to shrink to SDK-only too, (3) emptying + deleting the baseline file, (4) flipping static-quality-gate from ratchet mode to unconditional hard-deny so the boundary can never regress. Duplicate check 2026-07-06: pm-8778 (closed) built the gate mechanism; the 8 promotion items shrink the baseline domain-by-domain; searched 'boundary ratchet zero sdk-only imports capstone' — no existing item owns baseline-to-zero + gate hardening.

sdk-import-boundary-baseline.json reaches zero entries and is deleted; static-quality-gate hard-denies any private src/core import from src/cli/** and src/mcp/** with no baseline escape hatch; src/mcp handlers consume only src/sdk primitives; full test suite + 100% coverage gate green with zero CLI/MCP behavior change

message: Audit #10 2026-07-06: gap found while mapping pm-usfg children to the epic AC — nothing owned the terminal boundary flip.

value.text: Review-driven implementation evidence 2026-07-15: promoted the complete extension lifecycle engine and cohesive helpers from src/cli/commands/extension* into src/sdk/extension*, kept only thin CLI compatibility re-exports, and made SDK runtime/init consume the SDK implementation directly. This removed 45 additional grandfathered private-core edges: static quality reports actual_edge_count=597, baseline_edge_count=597, zero new/stale/dynamic violations. Suppression pruning reduced the mandatory budget 170 -> 167. Final all-source verification passes 291 files / 5,713 tests at exact 100%: 36,940 statements, 26,521 branches, 7,450 functions, 36,055 lines. The capstone remains incomplete because 597 edges remain, so it is returning to open.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Third-head review evidence: replaced per-destination extension install locking with one scope-wide transaction lock carrying a UUID owner token; stale and final cleanup re-read ownership before removal, and regressions prove cross-destination serialization, invalid/stale-owner refusal, replacement-owner preservation, and owner-write cleanup. Install activation now requires explicit runtime status ok. Executable aliases moved into SDK contracts so SDK scaffolding has no reverse CLI dependency. Directory normalization uses linear index scans, eliminating both CodeQL regex findings. Focused extension/bootstrap tests pass 216/216; repository verification passes 291 files / 5,717 tests at exact 100%: 36,962 statements, 26,531 branches, 7,453 functions, 36,077 lines. Typecheck, lint, duplicates, static/docstring/package/token gates pass; suppression budget improved 167 -> 166. Temp-directory package-first dogfood passed 88 real CLI/SDK/package commands.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Shared extension managed-state and settings mutations require scope-level serialization; destination-level locks protect copies but cannot protect shared snapshots. Persisted unique ownership must be re-read before stale reclaim and final release so an earlier installer cannot delete a replacement lock.

author: codex-root
message: Review-driven extension SDK boundary and integrity work verified; capstone remains open because the repository baseline is not yet zero

author: codex-root
message: Release after verified third-head review implementation

author: codex-root
message: Fourth-head review implementation: add owner-bound extension install lease heartbeats so active scope locks cannot expire mid-transaction

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Fourth-head CodeRabbit evidence: accepted the active-lock-expiry finding. Scope locks now run an owner-token-bound lease heartbeat at one-third of the stale interval; every tick re-reads current ownership before refreshing mtime, stop is an async barrier before final release, and heartbeat I/O failure remains best-effort without aborting the install. Regressions prove a transaction running beyond stale_ms retains exclusivity, replacement owners are never heartbeated or removed, and failed heartbeats do not break installs. Focused extension tests pass 124/124; full verification passes 291 files / 5,719 tests at exact 100%: 36,977 statements, 26,535 branches, 7,458 functions, 36,092 lines. Typecheck, strict quality gates, and 88-command temp-directory dogfood pass.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: A stale timeout is a lease duration, not an install duration: long-running owners must refresh liveness under the same persisted owner token, and cleanup must await any in-flight heartbeat before comparing and releasing ownership.

author: codex-root
message: Owner-bound lease heartbeat verified; capstone remains open because 597 boundary edges remain

Full release-grade verification including exact coverage packed smoke and temp dogfood

value.author: codex-wide-sdk-20260716
value.text: PR #573 review-hardening evidence: accepted and fixed projection honesty, blank numeric validation, complete highlight windows, internal search-flag stripping, safe unknown-error handling, missing-file stats reads, case-normalized duplicate keys, null-prototype user maps, strict numeric assertions, metadata-complete path deduplication, shell-wrapped recursion guards, positive/fractional timeout contracts, own-property JSON lookup, and project/global extension mismatch detection. Governance and update-many callers now explicitly request full list records. Two suggestions were intentionally rejected: public pm tracker links remain required by repository documentation policy, and unbounded Promise.all tracker reads would create unsafe fanout. Full release:gates passes: 292 files / 5,744 tests at exact 100% (37,260 statements, 26,520 branches, 7,536 functions, 36,360 lines), 100% docstrings, static/package-boundary/token/version/security gates, 12-package packed smoke, and 88-command temp dogfood. Boundary ratchet is clean at 51 source allowances / 412 edges with zero new, stale, or dynamic violations; therefore the zero-boundary capstone returns to open.

author: codex-wide-sdk-20260716

value.author: codex-wide-sdk-20260716
value.text: PR #573 exact-head bot follow-up: CI identified and pruned one stale ESLint suppression, lowering the mandatory ratchet 157 -> 156 across the gate, package script, and contract tests. DeepSource findings added purpose-specific docstrings to three governance projection/index helpers. CodeRabbit findings corrected the fractional run-timeout diagnostic and replaced the calendar-dependent 2030 list fixture with a runtime future date. Strict pnpm lint now passes with zero jscpd clones; static quality, 100% docstrings, package boundary, and token gates pass. Final exact coverage remains 292 files / 5,744 tests at 100/100/100/100 (37,260 statements, 26,520 branches, 7,536 functions, 36,360 lines). The new Gemini path-dedup comment was verified already resolved on head: buildLinkedTestKey includes normalized env_set, env_clear, pm_context_mode, shared_host_safe, and assertions for path tests.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Third exact-run review evidence: accepted CodeRabbit's merge-integrity finding so any failed active-child reparent now prevents the duplicate from closing; expanded the mutation-failure regression with two blocked children plus a no-child duplicate that still exercises close failure. Rejected Gemini's canonical-check removal after the existing prefixless-id regression proved raw parse validation cannot detect aliases that resolve to the canonical record. Rejected its JSON-path consolidation finding as stale: only execution.ts defines those helpers on this head and traversal already uses own-property checks. DeepSource serial analysis had advanced into normalize.ts, so every changed private/public normalize implementation now uses documented lexical exports/bindings, closure backfill is table-driven, and plan application is factored into bounded-complexity primitives without suppressions. Focused tests pass 27/27; strict lint/static/docstring/boundary/token and zero-duplicate gates pass; full coverage passes 292 files / 5,744 tests at exact 100/100/100/100 (37,344 statements, 26,444 branches, 7,571 functions, 36,433 lines). The mandatory ESLint suppression budget improved 156 -> 154. Focused tests pass 153/153; strict lint, zero duplicates, static/docstring/package-boundary/token gates pass; exact coverage passes 292 files / 5,745 tests at 100/100/100/100 (37,487 statements, 26,356 branches, 7,625 functions, 36,577 lines).
